So this is very contextual and that is that as we've heard many times he's one of the last companions of all to pass away and the reason for that is that when the prophet sallallah.aii wasallam came to Medina he was how old? 10 years old. He was just a little boy. But the prophet sallallah.aii wasallam made dua for anis and he made dua that Allah give him a long life. And so lived very very very long. Some estimate his lifespan to be over 120 years old. And so was one of the last of the companions to pass away. So first of all he was young. Second of all, he lived a very very long time and would later prove to be one of the most critical companions in the time of the why because many of the older companions passed away and it fell on his shoulders to narrate and to transmit the sunnah of the prophet sallall.aii alaihi wasallam in their place. And who said in this quote obviously he's speaking to the people at the very end of the time of the and the beginning of the time of and during that time around the year 60 after Hijra That's when all of the turmoil and political problems started to crop up and this is when there was a lot of chaos etc. And this is when innovation started to become you know more prevalent and introduced to the masses. And so in this context said to the people around him during those days, he said, He says, "You all are doing things today that are more insignificant than a strain a strand of hair. things that are so negligible, things that you're taking for granted, things that you look at them as being no big deal at all. Right? He says, "Yet these things you all are doing that you're overlooking, no big deal." Is Allah going to literally burn me in the flames of hell if I don't do it? Because if not, I'm not really interested." This is the key difference. The companions when they received a command, they looked at it as an opportunity. See, they want to worship Allah more. They want more. This is the one of the most prominent of his descriptions. Look at that description. He comes to humanity. Humanity is not practicing a religion. Humanity is doing whatever they want, right? Free reign to their free will. And so as a result, it's it's it's like living in the wild amongst wild animals. If you're strong, you might get ahead a little bit. If you are weak or meek, you will get eaten alive. And so the prophet sallallah.aii alaihi wasallam comes to humanity and Allah refers to that quote unquote freedom that they were experiencing as chains and bondage and slavery, right? And the Quran and the Sunnah and the light of revelation is releasing them. Releasing them from the chains that they are in. The companions saw the Quran and Sunnah to mean that that the Quran and Sunnah came to release me from slavery to anyone or anything other than Allah to emancipate me, to free me. And that is why they took up every single drop of it. And the most precious thing in the world to them was their relationship with Allah and their journey to Allah. And anything that would threaten that relationship or threaten to damage it or impede it, they would steer very clear from it. I'm not allowed to do this. Everything is haram. The haram bug, the haram police, right? All these buzzwords people use to make fun of the people that command the good and forbid the evil. The people that don't take a step forward without verifying with Allah and his messenger and the scholars of Islam, the representatives of Allah and his messenger. summary: This lecture discusses a hadith from Riyadul Saliheen about vigilance and God-consciousness, featuring a narration from Anas ibn Malik. The speaker contrasts the attitude of the Prophet's companions—who eagerly embraced every command from Allah and His messenger with a posture of "we hear and we obey"—with the modern Muslim attitude of questioning obligations and seeking loopholes. The lecture emphasizes how the companions viewed Islam as liberation from slavery and bondage, understanding the Quran and Sunnah as tools for emancipation and strengthening their relationship with Allah. The speaker addresses misconceptions about religious adherence, particularly regarding the role of women in Islam, tracing these negative stereotypes to Western colonial propaganda designed to undermine Muslim societies by removing women from their homes.
suggested_title: The Attitude of the Companions: From 'We Hear and Obey' to Modern Skepticism - A Hadith on Vigilance and God-Consciousness
hadith_chapter: Chapter of Vigilance of Allah's Observation
catchy_title: Why the Companions Said Yes Without Hesitation
